matrix_description: DEBUG/ITERATE matrix — runs ONLY the LLM_ASSISTED cell from matrix_extensibility_v2.json. DIRECT_MAPPING and RULE_ONLY are deterministic and fully validated by prior archives, so iterating on dashboard / observation / class2-transition fixes does not need to repeat them (saves ~70 min per iteration). Use matrix_extensibility_v2.json (the full 3-cell matrix) for final paper-archive runs that establish v1↔v2 comparability. All other settings (_recommended_per_trial_timeout_s=480, _recommended_ollama_model=gemma4:e4b, _required_operator_policy_changes) are inherited from v2 by reference — do not change them here without also changing v2.
_required_operator_policy_changes: global_constraints.llm_request_timeout_ms must be set to 120000 (vs canonical 8000) before this matrix can run; the LLM_ASSISTED cell asserts this via _policy_overrides. Restore to 8000 after.
_iteration_intent: Fast feedback loop for observation-payload / dashboard breakdown fixes. Each run captures only the LLM-side data (which is the sole place that varies) and skips the deterministic baseline cells.

cells.description: Axis A v2 LLM_ASSISTED only. Same scenario, fixture, and expected as the full v2 matrix; same _policy_overrides. Pass criteria identical (CLASS_1 + approved). Use the resulting digest's by_route_class breakdown plus per-trial class2 transition fields to analyse where the LLM's safe_deferral choices end up — the goal of running this iteratively is to make those downstream outcomes visible in the dashboard.
cells._policy_overrides_note: Inherited from matrix_extensibility_v2.json LLM_ASSISTED cell — see that file for the full operator runbook.

anchor_commits._purpose: Filled in by the orchestrator at sweep start.
